name: Ebony and Ivory Chocolate Truffles
recipeIngredient: 7 1/2 oz White chocolate, chopped into 1/4 inch pieces
8 oz Semi-sweet chocolate, chopped into 1/4 inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
EQUIPMENT: Cutting board, cook's knife, measuring cup, measuring spoons, 2 stainless steel bowls, 1 1/2-quart saucepan, 2 whisks, baking sheet, parchment paper.
1. Place the white and dark chocolates into separate stainless steel bowls.
2. Heat the cream in a 1 1/2 quart saucepan over medium heat. Bring to a boil.
3. Pour 1/4 cup of boiling cream over the white chocolate and the remaining 1/2 cup over the dark chocolate. Allow to stand for 4 to 5 minutes.
4. Stir each with a separate whisk until smooth, and allow to cool at room temperature for 1 hour.
5. Refrigerate the two ganaches for 15 minutes, stirring every 5 minutes.
6. Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
7. Portion 36 heaping teaspoons of dark chocolate into separate mounds onto the parchment paper.
8. Top each teaspoon of dark chocolate with a level teaspoon of white chocolate.
9. To fashion the truffles, roll each portion of chocolate in your palms using a gentle circular motion, applying just enough pressure to form smooth rounds.
10. Roll the rounds of chocolate in cocoa until completely covered.
